mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 7% triggers aggressive central bank tightening, which reduces corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or signal loses predictive power after 2023.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in unemployment and declines in payroll employment signal labor market deterioration, prompting monetary easing that boosts equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of triggered cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive short-term equity returns before macro headwinds materialize.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns in >60% of late-cycle periods, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales reflect robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive retail sales growth coincides with negative equity returns in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated inflation and rising policy rates creates a hostile financial environment, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns over the month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant, or forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite dual macro pressures.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth exceeding 300,000 signals labor market overheating, which forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, resulting in negative equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of cases when payroll growth exceeds 300k, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy post-2023.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts indicate weakening residential investment and broader economic slowdown, reducing future earnings expectations and driving negative equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Signal predicts positive returns in >60% of declining housing start periods, or 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with HOUST momentum.
